Mo For Court Order Compelling Pltf'S Health Care Providers To Produce Medical & Mental Health Records Of Pltf Jerome Esser Aka Roman Esser
Off Calendar Jun-20-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joan Edelsohn, for Defendant/Moving party
R. Boone Callaway, for Plaintiff/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion For Court Order Compelling Plaintiffs' Health Care Providers To Produce Medical and Mental Health Records Of Plaintiff is granted in part and denied in part. As served, the subpoenas are overly broad. The subpoenas are limited to records which set forth the diagnosis of bipolar disorder and borderline personality disorder, treatment of that bipolar disorder and borderline personality disorder condition 30 days prior to August 4, 2004, any treatment for that bipolar disorder and borderline personality disorder arising out of incident of August 4, 2004, and treatment of the area of the left clavicle from August 4, 2004 to present. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Order Compelling Plntfs' Health Care Providers To Produce Medical And Mental Health Records Of Plntf Jerome Esser Aka Roman Esser
Off Calendar May-23-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant SF PENDULUM, INC.'s Motion For Court Order Compelling Plaintiffs' Health Care Providers To Produce Medical and Mental Health Records is ordered off calendar for failure to serve the motion upon San Francisco General Hospital or MediCal/Medicare and its affiliates.
The motion is not properly brought pursuant to Civil Code Section 56.10(b)(1). Code of Civil Procedure Section 1985.7 is the applicable code section. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer as to the breadth of any subpoena. As presently written, counsel are advised that the documents being sought are overly broad. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Dec-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Richard Boone Callaway, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Joan Edelsohn, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel:
Granted in part, denied in part, and moot in part. The motion is moot as to Request No. 1 as the photographs have been produced. The motion is granted as the supplemental responses have not been verified. Verification shall be produced within 10 days of notice of this order. The motion is granted as to No. 2 as the response references documents dating after June 30, 2004, which is unresponsive. As to Nos. 2, except as it is unresponsive, and Nos. 3-5, the motion is denied as the supplemental responses are adequate, pending verification.
Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
